extraction_policy: Every memory and entity link must be supported by current narrative-unit text or extracted-candidate evidence.
Write all natural-language output values in the same language as the current narrative unit, except for exact names, codes, and schema labels.
Preserve the order of events and revelations within the current narrative unit.
Classify each memory_temporal_scope as temporal_episode, atemporal_fact, durable_state, or uncertain.
Use atemporal_fact for world rules, technical principles, stable setting facts, definitions, and background facts that only need reveal-time visibility after they are introduced.
Use durable_state for stable roles, beliefs, plans, obligations, relationships, or entity states that persist until updated.
Use temporal_episode for concrete actions, utterances, reveals, local observations, and ordered scene events.
Extract only memories that can affect later writing continuity, entity state, entity knowledge, or relationship reasoning.
Prefer 3 to 8 episodic memories for an ordinary narrative unit; use fewer when the unit is mostly visual or atmospheric.
Do not create episodic memories solely for open questions, because open questions are stored in scene context.
Do not duplicate the same fact as both an observation and a state_change unless an explicit before/after change appears in the source.
Link memories only to entities that appear in the extracted KG entity candidates for this unit.
Every evidence field must be an exact contiguous substring copied from the current narrative unit's title, subtitle, or content.
Do not use paraphrased evidence, translated evidence, shortened evidence with ellipses, or text copied only from extracted candidates.
Do not create entity links for ordinary body parts, broad generic concepts, or deictic phrases unless they are explicit extracted KG candidates.
Do not output relationship_observations in this task; durable relationships are extracted by a separate task.
The output must be parseable JSON.
